Observation 1: Based on the narrative, Sophia was present at the soccer field at the time of the murder. The text explicitly states: "Today, she'd been on the field, right where it all happened." This places her at the scene. For Larry, the narrative provides an alibi. It states that Larry was at Savannah's political rally, which was held "downtown at the center square." The murder occurred at a soccer field during a match's halftime, which is a different location and time. Therefore, Larry has an alibi for the time of the murder, while Sophia does not.

Thought 2: Sophia has a strong motive (Savannah threatened to reveal her source and ruin her credibility), opportunity (she was present at the scene), and means (she had a flamethrower from a military exhibition). Larry also has a motive (political rivalry) and means (access to a flamethrower), but he has an alibi placing him elsewhere at the time of the murder. Therefore, Sophia is the most likely murderer.
